nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Suffolk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from the county of Suffolk.

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla

It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk was born in 1170, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Sir Walford le Yonge. She died in 1240, in her hometown, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk est née en 1170, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Walford le Yonge. Elle est décédée en 1240, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk wurde 1170 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Walford le Yonge mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1240 im Alter von 70 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk è nata il nel 1170 nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Walford le Yonge. Morì nel 1240, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk(은)는 1170년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Walford le Yonge(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1240년 그녀의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sybil of Suffolk родилась в 1170 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Walford le Yonge. Она умерла в 1240 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.
